elements.description: Field used to enter the account username for authentication.
Masked field used to enter the account password.
Navigates back without authenticating the user.
Returns the user to the home page without authentication.
Generic authentication failure message displayed for invalid, blank, or partial credential submissions.

reviewSummary.summary: Core login controls are present, but the artifact contains duplicate password elements, noisy layout content, weak naming, brittle locators, and is missing the critical generic failed authentication validation element required by the workflow.

issues.message: Two separate password elements represent the same functional control using different DOM layers (native input and ion-input wrapper). This creates ambiguity for downstream automation and keyword generation.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep only one canonical password field element using the most stable and automation-friendly locator.

issues.message: Element name is implementation-oriented and inconsistent with workflow terminology.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename to 'username_field' for business-readable and reusable automation semantics.

issues.message: Element name is too generic and omits the field purpose/type context.
Locator uses autogenerated Ionic input id 'ion-input-0', which is likely unstable across builds and sessions.
issues.suggested_fix: Prefer a stable semantic locator such as placeholder, form-control identifier, accessible label, or stable component id.
Use stable password field identifiers such as 'id=password' or a semantic attribute-based locator.

issues.message: XPath locator depends on display text token '[BTN_LOGIN]' which may be localization-driven, dynamically rendered, or unstable.
issues.suggested_fix: Prefer stable attributes such as automation id, role/button text normalization, or semantic button metadata if available.

issues.message: Background content container is decorative/layout-only and not useful for workflow automation or validation.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the element from the approved page model.

issues.message: The workflow explicitly requires validation that all authentication failures display a generic Failed message, but no authentication failure message element exists in the artifact.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a reusable 'authentication_failed_message' or equivalent validation element with a stable locator.

issues.message: Password masking verification support is only indirectly represented through field type/style and lacks an explicit reusable verification-oriented element definition.
issues.suggested_fix: Ensure the canonical password field element retains a stable locator and supports validation of masked input behavior through type=password or equivalent property.
